characters.Name: Arlia, Destined Trainee
characters.Flavor: A young girl with incredible potential. Hailing from a small village called Urnakle, she entered the trainee program with a chip on her shoulder and a desire to make her people proud.

characters.Name: Mage Arlia
characters.Ability Text: When Mage Arlia enters play, deal 10 damage to each of your other Mages Guild characters. While she is in play, your Mages Guild characters have +10 ATK.
characters.Flavor: A young mage with incredible potential. She seeks greater strength to protect those she loves.

characters.Name: Squire Arlia
characters.Ability Text: Once per your opponent's turn and when an attack is declared at an individual target, redirect the attack to this unit and grant this unit +10 DEF during damage calculation.

characters.Name: Arlia, Youngest Archmage
characters.Affiliations: Mages Guild, The Archive, Destined, Attuned
characters.Ability Text: If your opponent targets a card you control with a destroying effect, negate it and destroy it.
characters.Flavor: The youngest Archmage in centuries with abilities and wisdom far beyond her years.

characters.Name: Captain Arlia of the Royal Army
characters.Affiliations: Kaethlaan Knights, Royal Army, King's Court (Kaethlaan), Destined
characters.Ability Text: If King Honathan is in play, +30 ATK and DEF, this character cannot be targeted by manipulation attempts and this character cannot change control.
characters.ChainFormula: Deal damage equal to the sum of all ATK +30.
characters.Flavor: The Captain of the Royal Army. She fights with boldness and has sworn lifelong fealty to the crown.
characters.Notes: Royal Army affiliation added this session

characters.Name: The Wandering Acolyte Arlia
characters.Ability Text: Once per turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and arrange them in any order.
characters.Flavor: A former Mages Guild member who has become disillusioned. She searches for truth greater than theirs.

characters.Name: The Ascended
characters.Ability Text: This character's HP, ATK, and DEF are the number of items discarded during transformation x20. Stat-modifying riders on consumed items do not apply; ability-grant riders still apply.
characters.TransformIn: The Wandering Acolyte Arlia + 1+ T3 Items
characters.ChainFormula: Deal damage to all characters on both sides of the field except chained characters equal to the sum of ATK x2
characters.ChainExtras: Characters defeated by this attack are Disgraced.
characters.Flavor: A former mage once called Arlia who found the greatest truth of the universe. She communes with Gods and does not observe human needs.

characters.Name: Arlia, Kael's Wife
characters.Ability Text: TBD - married synergy with Kael, Arlia's Husband
characters.TransformIn: Arlia (T2) + Kael at T2/T3 (marriage / paired transformation)
characters.Flavor: TBD
characters.Notes: Marriage form. Tier/stats/ability/flavor not yet designed.

characters.Name: Kael, Destined Trainee
characters.Flavor: A street boy with a desire for revenge on those who looked down on his family.
characters.Notes: Vanilla aggressive base; all offense and no guard.

characters.Name: Swiftblade Kael
characters.Flavor: One of the most gifted swordsmen in the entire Royal Army; cynical and aggressive.
characters.Notes: Hub form; vanilla beater. Highest base ATK at T2. Branches into the Loyal, Outlaw, and Subordinate roads.

characters.Name: Kael the Shadow
characters.Ability Text: Kael the Shadow may declare attacks against opposing passive-zone characters, not only the active zone. While King Honathan is in play, Kael the Shadow has +10 ATK.
characters.Flavor: A royal army assassin who reports directly to King Honathan. Quiet and precise.
characters.Notes: Loyal road. Precision = backline reach; escalates to Leader reach on The King's Blade.

characters.Name: The King's Blade
characters.Ability Text: The King's Blade may declare an attack against any opposing character or the opposing Leader, ignoring zone restrictions. While King Honathan is in play, The King's Blade has +20 ATK, and when The King's Blade KOs a character, draw a card.
characters.Flavor: He has no name now — only an edge, and a hand the crown trusts to hold it.
characters.Notes: Loyal-road terminal. Honathan's +10/+10 aura plus this rider stack; can snipe the Leader past defenders. Power-approved.

characters.Name: Kael the Captured
characters.Flavor: A former royal soldier captured by enemy during war. Biding his time to escape.
characters.Notes: Outlaw-road entry; diminished POW body, vanilla. Gated on Taken Prisoner (playable only while a War is in play). War-Torn (cannot attack) comes only from Taken Prisoner, not from the War cards themselves.

characters.Name: Kael the Runaway
characters.Ability Text: Kael the Runaway cannot benefit from your affiliation auras.
characters.Flavor: A former royal soldier who escaped to chase his own fate.
characters.Notes: Outlaw road. Escape = a normal transformation (no extra event needed). Self-reliant: no aura benefit.

characters.Name: Kael the Killer
characters.Ability Text: Kael the Killer gains +10 ATK for each kill he has banked (kills persist across transformations). Kael the Killer cannot benefit from your affiliation auras.
characters.Flavor: A notorious criminal who kills for the highest bidder.
characters.Notes: Outlaw road. Uncapped kill-scaling, but DEF 0 and fragile. Power-approved.

characters.Name: The Silent
characters.Ability Text: The Silent must be declared as an attacker each combat phase if able. The Silent may target opposing passive-zone characters, and deals +20 damage to characters with ATK 50 or higher. The Silent cannot benefit from your affiliation auras.
characters.Flavor: The most renowned swordsman in all the land, seeking a worthy opponent.
characters.Notes: Outlaw-road terminal. Highest base ATK in the game (90); forced-attack downside. Power-approved.

characters.Name: Kael, Arlia's Husband
characters.Ability Text: TBD - married synergy with Arlia, Kael's Wife
characters.TransformIn: Swiftblade Kael + Arlia at T2 (marriage / paired)
characters.Flavor: A boy from the streets with a changed perspective on life. Says every day is a gift.
characters.Notes: Love road; terminal at peace. Exits to Mourning Widower if wife dies.

characters.Name: Mourning Widower, Kael
characters.Ability Text: TBD
characters.TransformIn: Kael, Arlia's Husband (when Arlia, Kael's Wife dies)
characters.Flavor: With his wife dead, Kael has sworn to avenge her. He fights with abandon as he has nothing left to lose.
characters.Notes: Love road; terminal dead-end

characters.Name: Second in Command Kael
characters.Ability Text: While you control Arlia, you may redirect attacks declared at Arlia to Second in Command Kael, and Second in Command Kael has +10 ATK and +10 DEF.
characters.TransformIn: Swiftblade Kael, while you control Arlia at T3 or higher (Arlia is not consumed)
characters.Flavor: Arlia's second and confidant. He is loyal to a fault and refuses to consider disobedience.
characters.Notes: Subordinate-road T2 terminal. Presence-gated transform — no paired-transform rule needed. Bodyguard-redirect echoes Squire Arlia's Me for You.

characters.Name: King Honathan of Kaethlaan
characters.Affiliations: Royal Army, Kaethlaan Knights, King's Court (Kaethlaan)
characters.Ability Text: Play requirement: you control at least two other Kaethlaan characters. While King Honathan is in play, your King's Court (Kaethlaan) characters have +10 ATK. Leader rule: while you control a Royal Army character, King Honathan cannot be attacked.
characters.ChainFormula: Deal damage equal to the sum of all ATK +20.
characters.Flavor: Forty years he has kept the hearth lit. Pray you never learn what it cost him.
characters.Notes: First non-Arlia character. Standalone T4 capstone (no transform-in). Affiliations and ability key off Royal Army; also a Kaethlaan Knight and head of the King's Court. Activates Captain Arlia's My Liege.

characters.Name: Thestral
characters.Flavor: Only those who have seen death can see them coming.
characters.Notes: Single-form wild creature; cheap Dark body for filler/opposition and Light-vs-Dark testing.

characters.Name: Lor'oak Goblin Grunt
characters.Flavor: Numbers were always Lor'oak's only plan.
characters.Notes: Base form of the T2-terminal tribal line; cheap fast play. Upgrade path printed on the Commander.

characters.Name: Lor'oak Goblin Commander
characters.ChainFormula: Deal damage equal to the sum of all ATK vs target's DEF.
characters.Flavor: Forward. Always forward. There are always more.
characters.Notes: T2 terminal; canonical low-tier chain example from Combat & Effects. Validates creatures-as-Leaders and lowest-tier chains.

characters.Name: Illyego, the Orphan
characters.Ability Text: Illyego may attack even while War-Torn.
characters.Flavor: He does not remember a year without a war in it.
characters.Notes: Common side-character line; attack-through-War-Torn is his identity. Needs an involuntary War-Torn source to matter (Holy War coin-flip capture).

characters.Name: Illyego, the Soldier
characters.Ability Text: Illyego may attack even while War-Torn.
characters.TransformIn: Illyego, the Orphan (only while a War is in play)
characters.Flavor: They handed him a spear before he could spell his name.
characters.Notes: Child-soldier phase. Conscription-gated: transform only while a War is in play.

characters.Name: Illyego, the Conqueror
characters.Ability Text: Illyego may attack even while War-Torn. Illyego, the Conqueror has +10 ATK while a War is in play.
characters.TransformIn: Illyego, the Soldier (after Illyego has banked 3 kills)
characters.Flavor: He stopped waiting for the war to end and made it his.
characters.Notes: Terminal. Self-made warlord, not a hireling - Faithless (his own master), not Mercenary. Kill-gated transform.

characters.Name: The Acolyte Illyego
characters.Ability Text: The Acolyte Illyego cannot become War-Torn.
characters.Flavor: He buried the spear at a shrine and did not dig it up.
characters.Notes: Terminal redemption branch (Light). Reuses the Disillusioned wanderer gate. Sheds War Child - he has left war behind.

characters.Name: Goblin Soldier
characters.Flavor: One of many. That is the point.
characters.Notes: Common Goblin body; generic rank line (not Lor'oak). Base of Soldier -> Lieutenant -> Captain.

characters.Name: Goblin Lieutenant
characters.Flavor: Promoted by surviving.
characters.Notes: Mid-rank generic Goblin.

characters.Name: Goblin Captain
characters.Ability Text: While Goblin Captain is in play, your other Goblin characters have +10 ATK.
characters.Flavor: He counts heads before a battle and after. The numbers rarely match.
characters.Notes: Terminal generic-Goblin commander; aura only (chains stay special to the Lor'oak line).

characters.Name: Barangrang
characters.Flavor: Gone before you finish blinking.
characters.Notes: Common Wild creature; single-form T1 terminal.

characters.Name: Murlifect
characters.Ability Text: At the start of your turn, Murlifect heals 10.
characters.Flavor: Take a piece; it grows two back.
characters.Notes: Common Wild creature; sticky regen body.

characters.Name: Tidewhisk
characters.Flavor: It laughs in a language of bubbles.
characters.Notes: Common Wild creature.

characters.Name: Glimmermoth
characters.Flavor: Drawn to every lantern; burned by none.
characters.Notes: Common Wild creature; cheap defensive body.

characters.Name: Bogfang
characters.Flavor: The swamp grew teeth.
characters.Notes: Common Wild creature; aggressive.

characters.Name: Pyrnit
characters.Flavor: Small, furious, briefly.
characters.Notes: Common Wild creature; glass-cannon.

characters.Name: Frostnip
characters.Flavor: A cold little nip in the dark.
characters.Notes: Common Wild creature.

characters.Name: Stoneback
characters.Flavor: Patience with a shell.
characters.Notes: Common Wild creature; cheap wall.

characters.Name: Lumenkit
characters.Ability Text: When Lumenkit enters play, heal one of your characters 10.
characters.Flavor: A small warmth that follows you home.
characters.Notes: Common Wild creature; tiny support.

characters.Name: Touched Child Hresheeba
characters.Flavor: A child who answers questions no one asked aloud.
characters.Notes: Base of Hresheeba's 2-tier line (kept short so the enabler lands fast). Upgrades to Old Maid Hresheeba.

characters.Name: Old Maid Hresheeba
characters.Ability Text: While Old Maid Hresheeba is in play, your Divine Channel chains may be initiated with one fewer participant (minimum 1), and your Divine Channel characters have +10 ATK.
characters.Flavor: She has kept the door so long she has forgotten which side she is on.
characters.Notes: 2-tier terminal enabler (fast to deploy). Second Divine Channel body and reduces Divine Channel chain size by 1, so The Channel can assemble (or fire solo). Fetchable by Call of the Channel. Fixes assembly, not The Ascended's arrival.

characters.Name: Channel Being
characters.Flavor: Not a person. A doorway that learned to stand up.
characters.Notes: Generic Divine Channel body (run multiples) to assemble The Channel chain. Single-form T1; spared by Holy War (Light).

characters.Name: A Man Bred for War
characters.Ability Text: You may play A Man Bred for War from your hand as a standalone T3 body. While A Man Bred for War is War-Torn, he may attack, and he has +20 ATK and +20 DEF.
characters.Flavor: They made him for one thing. Then they were surprised by it.
characters.Notes: War-Torn payoff: capture him (Taken Prisoner / Holy War) to turn the downside into a buff. Standalone T3 — now has a printed play permission (balance-log Round 1) so he is castable; stats buffed 60/40/30 -> 60/50/40.

characters.Name: Embermaw
characters.Ability Text: When Embermaw enters play, deal 10 damage to one opposing active-zone character.
characters.Flavor: The coal grew a mouth.
characters.Notes: T2 Wild terminal reached only via Metamorphosis; entry burn. Added this session.

characters.Name: Craghide
characters.Ability Text: At the start of your turn, Craghide heals 10.
characters.Flavor: Older than the road that bends around it.
characters.Notes: T2 Wild terminal; sticky wall reusing the Regrow heal-over-time. Added this session.

characters.Name: Skirrl
characters.ChainFormula: Deal damage equal to the sum of all ATK vs target's DEF.
characters.Flavor: A gust that picked a direction.
characters.Notes: T2 Wild terminal; lowest-tier Wild chain (Chain 2 Wild active only). Added this session.

characters.Name: Tidewretch
characters.Flavor: It remembers being a river.
characters.Notes: T2 Wild terminal; balanced body. Added this session.

characters.Name: Hollowed Stag
characters.Ability Text: When Hollowed Stag enters play, heal one of your characters 10.
characters.Flavor: Antlers like a chapel with no roof.
characters.Notes: T2 Wild terminal; entry-heal support. Added this session.

characters.Name: Gravecreep
characters.Flavor: What the battlefield keeps.
characters.Notes: T2 Wild terminal; fragile aggro. Added this session.

characters.Name: Kaethlaan Recruit
characters.Flavor: Green, but the wall has to start somewhere.
characters.Notes: Royal Army climb base; loves Honathan's aura.

characters.Name: Kaethlaan Knight
characters.Flavor: The realm's spine, one shield at a time.
characters.Notes: Mid of the Royal Army soldier line.

characters.Name: Sword of the Realm
characters.Flavor: What a kingdom points when it has run out of words.
characters.Notes: Royal Army soldier capstone.

characters.Name: Channel Adept
characters.Ability Text: On enter, heal one of your characters 10.
characters.Flavor: The doorway learned a second word: mercy.
characters.Notes: Fixes the Channel Being dead end; entry heal.

characters.Name: Strango, Knight Trainer
characters.Ability Text: Chain ability.
characters.ChainFormula: Deal damage equal to the sum of all ATK.
characters.Flavor: He never lost a recruit he could shout at in time.
characters.Notes: Royal Army chain enabler (Chain 2 Royal Army).

characters.Name: Brutal Fighter Strango
characters.Ability Text: Enters War-Torn but may still attack; +20 ATK and DEF while War-Torn.
characters.Flavor: The trainer found the war he had only described.
characters.Notes: War-Torn payoff body (forged_in_chains).

characters.Name: Kaethlaan Archer
characters.Ability Text: May attack characters in the passive zone.
characters.Flavor: The back line is not as safe as it thinks.
characters.Notes: Ranged (hit_passive).

characters.Name: Kaethlaan Sniper
characters.Ability Text: May attack the passive zone; +20 ATK against targets with 50 or more ATK.
characters.Flavor: One breath. One name. One arrow.
characters.Notes: hit_passive + high_atk_bonus.

characters.Name: Thomas, Scared Recruit
characters.Flavor: He signed up for the bread.
characters.Notes: Base of the Thomas courage arc.

characters.Name: Soldier Thomas
characters.Flavor: He stopped counting the exits.
characters.Notes: Mid of the Thomas arc.

characters.Name: Thomas the Brave
characters.Ability Text: Cannot become War-Torn.
characters.Flavor: The bread was never the reason. He just hadn't met it yet.
characters.Notes: Courage capstone; cannot_become_wartorn.

characters.Name: Crator, Goblin Soldier
characters.Flavor: A grunt who survived enough battles to earn a name.
characters.Notes: Named generic Goblin body (T1 single-form). Legal diversity so the Goblin deck need not run 5x Goblin Soldier under the 3-copy cap. Slightly tougher than the common (30 HP). Added 2026-06-25.

characters.Name: Krakos, Goblin Archer
characters.Ability Text: May attack characters in the passive zone.
characters.Flavor: He prefers the fights he can end from a distance.
characters.Notes: Goblin ranged body (Wind element); hit_passive via effects-map. Gives the Goblin deck reach + legal diversity under the 3-copy cap. Added 2026-06-25.

characters.Name: Hierophant Vossuth
characters.Ability Text: While Hierophant Vossuth is in play, your Divine Channel chains may be initiated with one fewer participant (minimum 1).
characters.Flavor: He has held the keys so long he believes he forged the door.
characters.Notes: Channelian Church clergy; keeper_channel. Added 2026-06-25.

characters.Name: Hierophant Maredd
characters.Ability Text: While Hierophant Maredd is in play, your Channelian Church characters have +20 DEF.
characters.Flavor: The faithful tithe their strength; he keeps it safe.
characters.Notes: Channelian Church clergy; aura_church_def. Added 2026-06-25.

characters.Name: Hierophant Calyx
characters.Ability Text: When Hierophant Calyx enters play, heal one of your characters 10.
characters.Flavor: The rite still works. She is no longer sure why.
characters.Notes: Channelian Church clergy; entry heal_lowest. Added 2026-06-25.

characters.Name: Hierophant Ysmene
characters.Ability Text: While Hierophant Ysmene is in play, your Channelian Church characters have +10 ATK, and your Divine Channel characters that are NOT Channelian Church have -10 ATK.
characters.Flavor: She preaches loudest where the connection is faintest.
characters.Notes: Channelian Church clergy - most corrupt; aura_church_atk + aura_suppress_natural. Added 2026-06-25.

characters.Name: Seremin the Sickly
characters.Ability Text: When Seremin the Sickly enters play, you may play Plague from your hand or deck. Seremin the Sickly is immune to Plague effects.
characters.Flavor: He should have died with his village. He never does.
characters.Notes: Plague archetype base + carrier + ideal Leader. plague_immune.

characters.Name: Seremin the Plaguebearer
characters.Ability Text: Seremin the Plaguebearer is immune to Plague effects. While Plague is in play, opposing characters have an additional -10 Max HP. Seremin the Plaguebearer has +40 DEF while Plagued.
characters.Flavor: The coughing stopped the day he stopped fighting it.
characters.Notes: plague_immune + plagued_def_40 (always-on).

characters.Name: Patient Zero Seremin
characters.Ability Text: Patient Zero Seremin is immune to Plague effects and may receive equipment of any type or tier. Patient Zero Seremin has +60 DEF while Plagued.
characters.Flavor: They stamped a number under his name.
characters.Notes: plague_immune + plagued_def_60 + bears_any_tier.

characters.Name: Experiment 2615
characters.Ability Text: Experiment 2615 is immune to Plague effects. While Plague is in play it has +80 DEF and heals 20 each turn; while Plague is not in play it cannot attack.
characters.Flavor: It answers to nothing.
characters.Notes: T4 apex; plague_immune. Conditional immunity/scaling NOT modeled (PRE-SIM stub).

characters.Name: Experiment 4432, Stage 1
characters.Ability Text: Not immune to Plague effects.
characters.Flavor: Cognition declining. Mass increasing.
characters.Notes: Plague body/fodder.

characters.Name: Experiment 4432, Stage 2
characters.Ability Text: Must attack each combat if able; cannot move to passive. Immune to Plague effects.
characters.Flavor: Purely appetite.
characters.Notes: plague_immune + must_attack (stub: must_attack not wired for non-Silent).

characters.Name: Experiment 4423A, Stage 1
characters.Ability Text: Not immune to Plague effects.
characters.Flavor: More aggressive. Less of everything else.
characters.Notes: Plague glass-cannon body.

characters.Name: Experiment 4423A, Stage 2
characters.Ability Text: Must attack the biggest threat; no heal. Immune to Plague effects.
characters.Flavor: It charges the biggest thing in the room.
characters.Notes: plague_immune.

characters.Name: Plagued Person, Stage 1
characters.Ability Text: Sustained-by creature: only playable while a Max HP source raises it above 0. Not immune to Plague.
characters.Flavor: Ambulatory. Barely.
characters.Notes: Sustained-by the O'Donner Research Lab (+30 Max HP). O'Donner Research affil so the Lab reaches it.

characters.Name: Plagued Person, Stage 2
characters.Flavor: The fever has a shape now.
characters.Notes: Mid fodder.

characters.Name: Plagued Person, Stage 3
characters.Ability Text: Cannot receive healing.
characters.Flavor: No stage four.
characters.Notes: Final fodder.

characters.Name: Dr. Abigail Venner
characters.Ability Text: Play only while Plague is in play and you control O'Donner Research Lab.
characters.Flavor: She sees data points that scream.
characters.Notes: Doctor; lock chain NOT modeled. Castable via CHAR_PLAY approximation.

characters.Name: Dr. Mark Poultrain
characters.Ability Text: While Dr. Mark Poultrain is in play, your Plagued characters have +30 DEF.
characters.Flavor: The results
characters.Notes: never.

characters.Name: Chris O'Donner
characters.Ability Text: While Chris O'Donner is in play, your O'Donner Research characters have +20 ATK.
characters.Flavor: Only one facility has a budget.
characters.Notes: plague_immune + aura_odonner (+20 ATK to your O'Donner Research). Standalone capstone.

characters.Name: St. Faechious
characters.Ability Text: While St. Faechious is in play, ALL your Divine Channel characters have +20 ATK and may chain from any zone; your Channelian Church suppression of naturals does not apply.
characters.Flavor: He will not let them be divided.
characters.Notes: aura_faechious - unites both wings. Standalone capstone.

characters.Name: Goblin Standard-Bearer
characters.Ability Text: While Goblin Standard-Bearer is in play, your Goblin characters have +10 ATK and +10 DEF for every two Goblins you control.
characters.Notes: They do not follow the standard. They follow what the standard means: there are more of us than of you.
__parsed_extra: Goblin go-wide PAYOFF (Round 19) — a SCALING horde anthem on a cheap T1 body (so it's a Goblin that counts toward its own count, not a slot-tax event). +10 ATK/DEF to ALL your Goblins per 2 Goblins you control (6 Goblins = +30/+30). Grows with the swarm; rewards going wide; the +DEF is the survivability the fragile horde lacks. Does NOT stack with a 2nd Bearer (boolean aura). Kill it to drop the buff. Ability flag aura_warband (stats.ts effAtk/effDef). Added this session.

characters.Name: Cinderpel
characters.Ability Text: When Cinderpel enters play, draw a card.
characters.Flavor: A coal that learned to run.
characters.Notes: Wild cantrip (R20): draws on enter (entry draw_card) — flood-and-churn fuel for the fusion combo. Added this session.

characters.Name: Sootcrawler
characters.Ability Text: When Sootcrawler enters play, search your deck for a Primal Fusion, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
characters.Flavor: It lives where the fire has been.
characters.Notes: Wild cantrip/tutor (R20): fetches a Primal Fusion on enter (entry fetch_fusion) — reliable fusion access. Added this session.

characters.Name: Galewing
characters.Ability Text: When Galewing enters play, draw a card.
characters.Flavor: It rides the storm it makes.
characters.Notes: Wild cantrip (R20): draws on enter (entry draw_card). Added this session.

items.Name: Instructional Tome
items.Text: Equipped character gains +10 ATK.
items.Flavor: A simple primer for the training of mages.
items.Notes: Gates Arlia, Destined Trainee -> Mage Arlia

items.Name: Instructional Sword
items.Text: Equipped character gains +10 ATK.
items.Flavor: A simple sword for the training of knights.
items.Notes: Gates Arlia, Destined Trainee -> Squire Arlia

items.Name: Reagent Pouch
items.Text: When you play this item, draw a card.
items.Flavor: A little of everything, in case the world asks for it.
items.Notes: Neutral fuel

items.Name: Apprentice's Grimoire
items.Text: When consumed in a transformation, the resulting character enters with +10 ATK.
items.Flavor: The second book is always heavier than the first.

items.Name: Squire's Oathblade
items.Text: When consumed in a transformation, the resulting character enters with +10 DEF.
items.Flavor: Sworn steel remembers every vow it was forged under.

items.Name: Staff of Aelion
items.Text: Equipped character gains +20 ATK, and an additional +10 ATK if their element is Fire.
items.Flavor: Crafted by the Stewards of Aelion, this staff is particularly attuned to fire mages.

items.Name: Carrion Blade
items.Text: Equipped character gains +10 ATK.
items.Flavor: A blade designed for protecting the living from the hungry dead.

items.Name: Archmage's Focus
items.Text: When consumed in a transformation, the resulting character enters with +20 ATK.
items.Flavor: Forged for a hand that has stopped trembling.

items.Name: Relic of the Forsaken
items.Text: When consumed in a transformation, the resulting character enters with +10 to all stats.
items.Flavor: Someone gave up everything to make this. It remembers the trade.

items.Name: Banner of the Realm
items.Text: When consumed in a transformation, the resulting character gains the chain ability "Rally - Chain 2 Kaethlaan Knights. Damage equals sum of ATK."
items.Flavor: It is not the cloth they follow. It is the idea the cloth insists upon.

items.Name: Feliefnir
items.Text: Equipped character gains +30 HP, +30 ATK, and +10 DEF. Equipped character cannot be targeted by opponent's equipped card effects.
items.Flavor: A legendary tome that is said to kill lesser magic users.

items.Name: Protection of The Divine
items.Text: Equipped character cannot be affected by item or event effects, whether friendly or hostile. If the equipped character is Disgraced, this item is destroyed.
items.Notes: RECONSTRUCTED from v0.7 session (was not in the prior project CSV). Mutual immunity (also blocks your own item/event effects on the bearer) is INTENTIONAL, not an oversight. Destruction-on-Disgrace is a functional downside clause (items are directly destroyable per the locked ruling). CONFIRM exact wording and flavor.

items.Name: Back-Alley Blade
items.Text: Equipped character gains +10 ATK.
items.Flavor: The blade a street boy taught himself to kill with — the Army only noticed later.
items.Notes: Gates Kael, Destined Trainee -> Swiftblade Kael. Kael's T1 weapon-gate, parallel to Instructional Sword/Tome.

items.Name: Warmonger's Resolve
items.Text: Play only while a War is in play. Equipped character has +20 ATK while a War is in play.
items.Flavor: Some men are only at peace when the world is not.
items.Notes: Wartime-buff family (any-war, open). Stat +20 ATK. Power to be settled by playtest.

items.Name: Unbroken Will
items.Text: Equipped character cannot become War-Torn. If it is already War-Torn, it may attack anyway.
items.Flavor: Chains are a kind of question. He kept answering no.
items.Notes: Wartime-buff family. War-Torn tech: immunity + attack-through-War-Torn (the two spinoffs in one equip). Not war-gated, so it pre-empts capture. Softens War-Torn's cost by design - watch in playtest.

items.Name: Goblin War-Banner
items.Text: Equip only to a Goblin character. Equipped Goblin has +10 ATK. While a Goblin War is in play, it has +30 ATK instead.
items.Flavor: Raised by the biggest goblin who lived long enough to raise it.
items.Notes: Wartime-buff family (Goblin-locked + Goblin-War-specific scaling, +10 -> +30). Race-restricted equip.

items.Name: Sanctified Blade
items.Text: Equip only to a Light character. Equipped character has +10 ATK. While Holy War is in play, it cannot be targeted by Dark characters' attacks.
items.Flavor: Forged in a chapel, quenched in a war.
items.Notes: Wartime-buff family (Light-locked + Holy-War rider). Stat + non-stat protection vs Dark. Playtest the anti-Dark clause.

items.Name: Whetstone
items.Text: When consumed in a transformation, the resulting character enters with +10 ATK.
items.Flavor: A blade is just an opinion until it is sharp.
items.Notes: Cheap T1 transform fuel.

items.Name: Buckler
items.Text: When consumed in a transformation, the resulting character enters with +10 DEF.
items.Flavor: Small, round, and the reason he still has a face.
items.Notes: Cheap T1 transform fuel.

items.Name: Field Rations
items.Text: When you play this item, heal one of your characters 10.
items.Flavor: Hardtack and resolve.
items.Notes: Cheap on-play heal/sustain.

items.Name: Twin Daggers
items.Text: Equipped character gains +10 ATK and +10 DEF.
items.Flavor: Two answers to every question.
items.Notes: Modest balanced equip; no downside.

items.Name: Tidecaller's Pearl
items.Text: Equipped character gains +20 ATK, and an additional +10 ATK if their element is Water.
items.Flavor: It hums when the tide turns.
items.Notes: Water analog to Staff of Aelion.

items.Name: Tower Shield
items.Text: Equipped character gains +20 DEF and -10 ATK.
items.Flavor: You will not get past it. You will not get much done behind it either.
items.Notes: Defensive equip with an offense downside.

items.Name: Vital Charm
items.Text: Equipped character gains +20 HP and -10 ATK.
items.Flavor: It keeps the heart beating. It does not make the hand quicker.
items.Notes: HP equip with an offense downside.

items.Name: Berserker's Brand
items.Text: Equipped character gains +30 ATK and -20 DEF.
items.Flavor: The brand burns away everything but forward.
items.Notes: Aggro closer for fragile attackers; big offense for big exposure.

items.Name: Aegis Plate
items.Text: Equipped character gains +40 DEF and +20 HP, but has -20 ATK and cannot be declared as an attacker.
items.Flavor: A fortress you can wear. A fortress does not charge.
items.Notes: Pure wall with a hard downside.

items.Name: Warlord's Spoils
items.Text: When consumed in a transformation, the resulting character enters with +10 to all stats.
items.Flavor: To the strong, the leavings of the strong.
items.Notes: Strong T3 transform fuel; T3-gated.

items.Name: Kael's Tarnished Blade
items.Text: Equipped character gains +10 ATK. Equip only to a Kael character.
items.Flavor: A street boy's first real blade — notched, but his.
items.Notes: Signature weapon (Kael-only). Forge base of the Kael blade line.

items.Name: Kael's Honed Blade
items.Text: Equipped character gains +20 ATK and +10 DEF. Equip only to a Kael character.
items.Flavor: Whetted on every lesson the Army never meant to teach.
items.Notes: Forge from Kael's Tarnished Blade (cost: 1 item).

items.Name: Reaver's Edge
items.Text: Equipped character gains +40 ATK and +10 DEF, plus +10 ATK while a War is in play. Equip only to a Kael character.
items.Flavor: The blade stopped being a tool the day it learned his grief.
items.Notes: Forge from Kael's Honed Blade (cost: 2 items). War payoff.

items.Name: Arlia's Lance
items.Text: Equipped character gains +20 ATK and +10 DEF. Equip only to an Arlia character.
items.Flavor: Light given a shaft and a point.
items.Notes: Signature weapon (Arlia-only).

items.Name: Lance of the Archmage
items.Text: Equipped character gains +40 ATK and +10 DEF. Equip only to an Arlia character.
items.Flavor: She stopped reaching for the staff. The lance reached back.
items.Notes: Forge from Arlia's Lance (cost: 2 items).

items.Name: Khaneris Sword
items.Text: Equipped character gains +20 ATK and +10 DEF.
items.Flavor: A king's blade, before it was a legend.
items.Notes: Legendary lore weapon; forge base.

items.Name: The Great Sword of Khaneris
items.Text: Equipped character gains +40 ATK and +20 DEF.
items.Flavor: They do not name swords after the living.
items.Notes: Forge from Khaneris Sword (cost: 2 items).

items.Name: Rough Blade
items.Text: Equipped character gains +10 ATK.
items.Flavor: Cheap steel, honestly forged.
items.Notes: Generic forge base (offense ladder).

items.Name: Tempered Edge
items.Text: Equipped character gains +20 ATK and +10 DEF.
items.Flavor: Folded until it stopped arguing.
items.Notes: Forge from Rough Blade (cost: 1 item).

items.Name: Masterwork Blade
items.Text: Equipped character gains +40 ATK and +10 DEF.
items.Flavor: The smith signed this one.
items.Notes: Forge from Tempered Edge (cost: 2 items).

items.Name: Round Shield
items.Text: Equipped character gains +10 DEF.
items.Flavor: A door you can carry.
items.Notes: Generic forge base — feeds the existing Tower Shield -> Aegis Plate ladder.

items.Name: Goblin Shiv
items.Text: Equipped character gains +10 ATK. Equip only to a Goblin character.
items.Flavor: Found, not made.
items.Notes: Goblin signature forge base.

items.Name: Goblin Cleaver
items.Text: Equipped character gains +20 ATK and +10 DEF. Equip only to a Goblin character.
items.Flavor: Bigger goblin, bigger answer.
items.Notes: Forge from Goblin Shiv (cost: 1 item).

items.Name: Warboss' Maul
items.Text: Equipped character gains +40 ATK and +10 DEF. Equip only to a Goblin character.
items.Flavor: The last argument the warband ever needs.
items.Notes: Forge from Goblin Cleaver (cost: 2 items).

items.Name: Kaethlaan Banner
items.Text: Equip only to a Kaethlaan character. That character gains +10 DEF, and your Kaethlaan characters gain +10 ATK.
items.Flavor: Follow it, or explain to it why you didn't.
items.Notes: Kaethlaan-only equip; grants an army-wide +10 ATK aura.

items.Name: Kaethlaan Bow
items.Text: Equipped character gains +20 ATK.
items.Flavor: Reach is its own kind of armor.
items.Notes: Kaethlaan ranged equip.

items.Name: Kaethlaan Broadsword
items.Text: Equipped character gains +20 ATK and +10 DEF.
items.Flavor: Heavy enough to mean it.
items.Notes: Kaethlaan versatile equip.

items.Name: Royal Warrant
items.Text: When consumed in a transformation, counts as any required named item.
items.Flavor: The crown's signature opens most doors.
items.Notes: Wild named transform fuel — helps the Kael and Arlia gates.

events.Name: Disillusioned
events.Text: Attach to one of your characters; that character becomes Disillusioned. While attached, that character cannot benefit from your affiliation auras. Consumed when the attached character transforms along a chosen-branch (Disillusioned) path.
events.Flavor: The banners she once saluted are only cloth now.
events.Notes: Canonical gate for the chosen-branch / wanderer transform. With Opportunity, enables Mage Arlia -> The Wandering Acolyte Arlia. 'Disillusioned' is a STATE; other events that confer it satisfy the same transform condition.

events.Name: Opportunity
events.Text: Play only if you control a Disillusioned character. Take one additional transformation action this turn.
events.Flavor: Every locked door has a moment it forgets to stay locked.
events.Notes: Catalyst half of the wanderer turn; pairs with Disillusioned. Generic value: an extra transformation action.

events.Name: A Crisis of Faith
events.Text: Attach to one of your characters; that character becomes Disillusioned. When this enters, draw a card. While attached, that character has +10 ATK and -10 DEF.
events.Flavor: Doubt does not make her careful. It makes her reckless.
events.Notes: Alternate Disillusioned source with an aggressive rider; satisfies chosen-branch transform conditions.

events.Name: Cast Out
events.Text: Attach to one of your characters; that character becomes Disillusioned. While attached, that character loses all Kingdom-internal affiliations.
events.Flavor: The order does not exile its own. It simply stops saying their names.
events.Notes: Harshest Disillusioned source; strips institutional chains and auras. Satisfies chosen-branch transform conditions.

events.Name: The Long Road
events.Text: Attach to a Disillusioned character or a Wandering character. At the start of each of your turns, that character heals 10. While attached, that character cannot bear equipment.
events.Flavor: The road provides. The road also takes.
events.Notes: Wanderer-line payoff and canonical heal-over-time example. Does NOT confer the Disillusioned state.

events.Name: Plague
events.Text: While Plague is in play, reduce the Max HP of all characters on both sides by 10. Cap-only rule applies.
events.Flavor: It does not ask which side you are on.
events.Notes: Persistent event (formerly a field card). Both-sides reach is carried in the text, not a category. Canonical Mathematical Opposition example and the canonical fall trigger ('Falls while Plague is in play').

events.Name: Medical Advancement
events.Text: While Medical Advancement is in play, increase the Max HP of your side's characters by 10.
events.Flavor: An honest kingdom answers death with bookkeeping.
events.Notes: Persistent event (formerly a field card). 'Your side' reach is carried in the text. Emergent counter to Plague's Max HP math; does not negate Plague's fall-trigger role.

events.Name: Call of the Channel
events.Text: Discard a card, then search your deck for a card with The Divine Channel affiliation, reveal it, add it to your hand, and shuffle your deck.
events.Notes: RECONSTRUCTED from v0.7 session (was not in the prior project CSV). Affiliation-locked tutor with a real cost (discard) per the tutoring design rule. Controller-side; no (G). CONFIRM exact wording, any element tag, and flavor. Now has real targets: The Ascended, Old Maid Hresheeba, Channel Being.

events.Name: Shield Wall
events.Text: Choose a Kaethlaan Knight you control. While Shield Wall is in play, that character gains DEF equal to its DEF minus 30 (minimum +0).
events.Notes: RECONSTRUCTED from v0.7 session. Renamed from 'Rally' to avoid collision with Banner of the Realm's Rally chain. Persistent Event framing is a deliberate workaround for the undefined one-shot buff-duration rule (open v0.7 question). Integer (DEF-30) buff avoids half-DEF breaking the x10 scale. CONFIRM wording (base vs current DEF), whether the Type should read 'Persistent Event / Kaethlaan', and flavor.

events.Name: Taken Prisoner
events.Text: Play only while a War is in play. Attach to one of your characters; that character becomes War-Torn. While attached, that character cannot attack. Consumed when the attached character transforms along a War-Torn path.
events.Flavor: The war did not kill him. It did something slower.
events.Notes: Costed state-gate modeled on Disillusioned, now gated behind an active War (you take prisoners during wars). Confers the War-Torn state (cannot attack); gates Swiftblade Kael -> Kael the Captured. Deliberate capture; gates Kael's outlaw road (Holy War's coin-flip capture does not). Also captures your own A Man Bred for War to buff him, and feeds The Broken March's War-Torn count.

events.Name: War
events.Text: While War is in play, at the start of each turn, deal 10 damage to each character in an active zone on either side. Leaders are not in the active zone and are unaffected. After War has been in play through two of your turns, flip a coin at the start of each of your turns; on heads, War ends and is removed.
events.Flavor: It starts at a border and ends at a grave.
events.Notes: World-state War (carries the War keyword for 'while a War is in play' references). Active-zone-only attrition, both sides; Leaders exempt (separate slot). Self-removing burnout via coin flip from turn 2. No capture of its own. Anchor of the War family.

events.Name: Holy War
events.Text: When Holy War enters play, each player flips a coin; on heads, that player chooses one character they control, and it becomes War-Torn. While Holy War is in play, at the start of each turn, deal damage to each character in an active zone on either side: any character whose element includes Light (including Dark & Light hybrids) takes none; a character whose element is Dark and not also Light takes 20; all others take 10. Leaders are unaffected. After Holy War has been in play through four of your turns, flip a coin at the start of each of your turns; on heads, Holy War ends and is removed.
events.Flavor: Every banner swears it fights for heaven.
events.Notes: World-state Light War. Now a CAPTURING war: on entry each player coin-flips and on heads makes one of their own characters War-Torn - the involuntary-ish War-Torn source that makes attack-through-War-Torn (Illyego, Unbroken Will) matter. This coin-flip capture does NOT gate Kael's outlaw road (that's still Taken Prisoner only). Attrition: Light-including spared, pure-Dark 20, others 10. Holds longer than War (burnout from turn 4).

events.Name: Goblin War
events.Text: While Goblin War is in play, at the start of each turn, deal 10 damage to each non-Goblin character in an active zone on either side. Goblin characters and Leaders are unaffected. After Goblin War has been in play through two of your turns, flip a coin at the start of each of your turns; on heads, Goblin War ends and is removed.
events.Flavor: They do not take ground. They take everything, slowly.
events.Notes: Affiliation-scoped War (carries the War keyword). Damages non-Goblin active-zone characters on both sides; Goblins and Leaders exempt - a payoff for a Goblin deck rather than a smaller Holy War. Same burnout as War (from turn 2). Taken Prisoner works during it normally. Default: 10 damage; confirm.

events.Name: Crusade
events.Text: Play only while Holy War is in play. While Holy War is in play, your Light characters have +10 ATK and +10 DEF.
events.Flavor: The faithful do not fear the fire. They are the fire.
events.Notes: Wartime-buff family (Holy-War-specific, Light-locked). Stacks with Holy War sparing Light - intended crusader payoff; watch in playtest.

events.Name: Horde Frenzy
events.Text: Play only while a Goblin War is in play. While a Goblin War is in play, your Goblin characters have +10 ATK, and whenever one of your Goblin characters is KO'd, draw a card.
events.Flavor: More. There are always more.
events.Notes: Wartime-buff family (Goblin-War-specific, Goblin-locked). Stat + non-stat card draw on Goblin deaths (the expendable horde).

events.Name: Hardened Veterans
events.Text: Play only while a War is in play. While a War is in play, your Royal Army characters take no damage from War effects.
events.Flavor: They have buried every kind of friend. War is just weather now.
events.Notes: Wartime-buff family (any-war, Royal-Army-locked). Non-stat war-damage immunity - the strongest of the set; makes a war one-sided for a Royal Army deck. Flagged for balancing.

events.Name: Rally to War
events.Text: Play only while a War is in play. While a War is in play, your characters in an active zone have +10 ATK.
events.Flavor: The drum does not ask who wants to march.
events.Notes: Wartime-buff family (any-war, your-side). Active-zone aggression aura - rewards attacking into the war you started (those characters also eat the war's attrition).

events.Name: The Broken March
events.Text: Play only while a War is in play. While The Broken March is in play, your War-Torn characters may attack. Once per turn, if you control two or more War-Torn characters, you may have them attack one target together, dealing damage to that target equal to the sum of their ATK.
events.Flavor: The chains came off all at once.
events.Notes: War archetype finisher - the War-Torn chain the sim said the war deck lacked. Overrides War-Torn's cannot-attack and grants a War-Torn chain (sum of ATK to one target) when 2+ are out. Pairs with Taken Prisoner, Holy War captures, A Man Bred for War, Illyego. Turns War-Torn from pure cost into a build-around payoff.

events.Name: Metamorphosis
events.Text: Transform a T1 Wild you control into any T2 Wild in your hand. Does not use your transformation action. The new form keeps banked kills; its element becomes the new form's. Consumed on use.
events.Flavor: Everything wild is also becoming something.
events.Notes: One-shot Wild-deck enabler: the any-to-any morph (no fixed lineage) that reaches the six T2 Wild terminals. Does not count as the turn's transformation action, so you can morph and transform the same turn. Destination must be in hand. Future special cards may unlock other transformations the same way. Added this session.

events.Name: Close the Gates
events.Text: While in play, your Kaethlaan characters take no damage from World-state Wars.
events.Flavor: The walls remember every siege they outlasted.
events.Notes: Kaethlaan war-attrition immunity (parallel to Hardened Veterans).

events.Name: Reinforce the Front Lines
events.Text: Heal one of your Kaethlaan characters 20.
events.Flavor: Fresh legs, fed and angry.
events.Notes: One-shot Kaethlaan heal.

events.Name: War College
events.Text: Your Royal Army characters require one fewer item to transform.
events.Flavor: Doctrine is just other people's deaths, written down.
events.Notes: Transform-cost reducer for the Royal Army climb.

events.Name: Field Promotion
events.Text: Search your deck for a form one of your characters can transform into, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
events.Flavor: The fastest way up is to be the one they cannot spare.
events.Notes: Tutor: fetches the next form for a body you control. War-deck consistency.

events.Name: War Effort
events.Text: Search your deck for a Destined or Faithless character, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
events.Flavor: Everyone serves. Some simply have not been asked yet.
events.Notes: Tutor: fetches a Kael (Destined) or Illyego (Faithless) character.

events.Name: Warren Muster
events.Text: Search your deck for a Goblin character, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
events.Flavor: The warren always has another to send.
events.Notes: Tutor: fetches a Goblin character. Goblin-deck consistency; parallels War Effort (affiliation-locked, slot cost). Added this session.

events.Name: Call of the Wild
events.Text: Search your deck for a Wild character, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
events.Flavor: The wild does not answer a summons. It answers a hunger.
events.Notes: Tutor: fetches a Wild character. Field Promotion cannot fetch Wild forms (they advance only via Metamorphosis), so this is how a Wild deck assembles the specific T2 terminal it will morph into. Added this session.

events.Name: Conscription Order
events.Text: Search your deck for a Royal Army character, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
events.Flavor: The realm does not ask. It enlists.
events.Notes: Tutor: fetches a Royal Army character. Loyalist climb consistency beyond the Divine Channel package (Call of the Channel). Affiliation-locked, slot cost. Added this session.

events.Name: Truce
events.Text: No attacks can be declared until your next turn. (Your combat this turn and your opponent's next combat are both skipped.)
events.Flavor: A line drawn in the mud that both sides pretend to honor.
events.Notes: Protection / stall tool (balance-log Round 9). Pushes the win condition toward T4/terminals by letting late-game decks survive a rush; an aggressor never wants it (forfeits its own attack). Engine: sets skipCombat on both players. AI plays it defensively when out-boarded.

events.Name: Sanctuary
events.Text: Until your next turn, a character you control cannot be attacked.
events.Flavor: Some ground is older than the war and will outlast it.
events.Notes: Protection family (balance-log Round 10). Single-target untargetable shield — surgical complement to Truce; protects one key climber/terminal/Leader. Engine: Unit.shielded, cleared at controller's next turn; excluded from attack + chain targeting.

events.Name: Bulwark
events.Text: Until your next turn, a character you control has +30 DEF.
events.Flavor: Set the shields. Let them break on us.
events.Notes: Protection family (balance-log Round 10). Single-target +30 DEF — the cheaper mitigation; saves a body when raising DEF past the incoming hit is enough. Engine: Unit.tempDef, cleared at controller's next turn.

events.Name: O'Donner Research Lab
events.Text: While in play, your O'Donner Research characters have +30 Max HP.
events.Flavor: The sign still says Wellness Institute.
events.Notes: Plague engine anchor; +30 Max HP to your O'Donner Research (sustains fodder under Plague).

events.Name: Patient Intake
events.Text: Search your deck for an O'Donner Research or Plagued character, reveal it, add it to hand, then shuffle.
events.Flavor: Every door opens inward.
events.Notes: Plague character tutor (affil-locked).

events.Name: Grant Proposal
events.Text: Search your deck for an O'Donner Research persistent event, reveal it, add it to hand, then shuffle.
events.Flavor: The grant is approved.
events.Notes: Lab tutor (event tutor - sim approximates with Field Promotion; not wired as a char tutor).

events.Name: Dispel
events.Text: Destroy a persistent event an opponent controls.
events.Flavor: The world-state is not as permanent as it pretends.
events.Notes: Categorical persistent-event removal - the answer the engine lacked (strips Plague / the Lab / a War). Names no card (Mathematical Opposition). Added 2026-06-25.

events.Name: The Open Channel
events.Text: Play only while you control a Divine Channel character. While The Open Channel is in play, at the start of each of your turns, search your deck for a form one of your characters can transform into, reveal it, add it to your hand, then shuffle.
events.Flavor: The door, once opened, forgets how to close.
events.Notes: Divine-Channel consistency engine (Round 13). A slot-cost persistent tutor: repeatable next-form fetch so the deep apotheosis line (Arlia -> Mage -> Acolyte -> Ascended) and clergy climbs actually assemble. DC-LOCKED by the play-condition (only a Divine Channel deck reliably controls a DC character), so it speeds the climb to T3 for the deck that needs it WITHOUT a meta-wide consistency buff (a free global version sent climb decks to 56%+ and broke aggro). Costs a passive slot + a card. Engine: sim auto-fetches the highest-tier climbable body's next form in startOfTurn. Added this session.

events.Name: Seeping Doubt
events.Text: While Seeping Doubt is in play, at the start of each of your turns, choose a character you control and flip a coin; on heads, that character becomes Disillusioned.
events.Flavor: It does not argue. It only waits, and asks the same question every morning.
events.Notes: Repeatable coin-flip Disillusioned source (Round 13). A persistent engine for the wanderer / apotheosis transforms (Wandering Acolyte Arlia, Acolyte Illyego) when you don't draw a one-shot Disillusioned card. Slot-cost; ~50%/turn. Sim: at start of turn, targets a not-yet-Disillusioned body with a disillusion-gated transform reachable and flips p.rnd()<0.5. Added this session.

events.Name: Primal Fusion
events.Text: Discard a card. Then choose two Wild creatures you control; one (the Apex) absorbs the other, gaining its HP and ATK, and triggers an effect by the absorbed creature's element — Fire: 10 damage to an enemy; Water: heal 10; Earth: +10 DEF; Wind: +10 ATK; Light: heal an ally 10; Dark: 10 damage to the opposing Leader. An Apex that has absorbed 2 or more may attack the passive zone; 3 or more may attack the opposing Leader. Does not use your transformation action.
events.Flavor: The pack does not grow. It deepens.
events.Notes: Wild APEX mechanic (Round 20). Repeatable/chainable combo — keep feeding one Apex to stack ATK/HP (NOT DEF: a glass cannon) + element triggers + reach (×2 -> passive) and a Leader-strike (×3 -> hit_leader). COSTS a discard. Fueled by Wild cantrips (Galewing draw, Sootcrawler tutors a fusion) and nerfed T1 Wilds (fusion FUEL) — a flood-and-fuse combo deck. Tuned to ~53% (fusion fires ~59% of games; deep chains rarer). Engine: transform.ts fuse() + combat.ts (Unit.fusions). Added this session.

events.Name: Pile On
events.Text: Discard a card. Then choose two Goblin creatures you control; one (the Apex) absorbs the other, gaining its HP and ATK, and triggers an effect by the absorbed creature's element (as Primal Fusion). An Apex that has absorbed 2 or more may attack the passive zone; 3 or more may attack the opposing Leader. Does not use your transformation action.
events.Flavor: When enough goblins want the same thing, they become one bigger problem.
events.Notes: Goblin-locked twin of Primal Fusion — uses the SAME unified Apex engine (fuse(): gains ATK+HP not DEF, element effect by the absorbed creature's element, reach at 2 fusions / Leader-strike at 3, discard cost). In the card pool but NOT in the sample Goblin deck (go-wide Goblin prefers its scaling anthem + chains over concentrating bodies; fusion is Wild's identity). Round 19/20.
